assertions.expectation: Clearly states that GKE auto-upgrades clusters automatically as the default and recommended model — this is what differentiates GKE from self-managed Kubernetes
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'GKE will handle upgrades automatically — this is the recommended approach and one of GKE's key value propositions' and explains this is different from manual management

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows to control WHEN auto-upgrades happen (e.g., weekends, off-peak hours)
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command to configure maintenance windows during off-peak hours with weekly Sunday schedule example

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance exclusions to control WHAT auto-upgrades happen (e.g., 'no minor or node upgrades' to block disruptive changes while allowing security patches)
assertions.reason: Includes specific section on 'Minor Version Control' with gcloud command to add maintenance exclusions for 'no_minor_upgrades' while allowing security patches

assertions.expectation: Does NOT recommend disabling auto-upgrades or switching to a purely manual upgrade cadence as the first option
assertions.reason: Response consistently recommends auto-upgrades throughout and only mentions manual upgrades for specific exceptional scenarios

assertions.expectation: Explains that manual upgrades are useful for specific scenarios (emergency patches, accelerating the schedule) but are the exception, not the rule
assertions.reason: Includes dedicated section 'When to Use Manual Upgrades' listing specific scenarios like emergency patches and testing, clearly positioning them as exceptions

assertions.expectation: Recommends enrolling in scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance) for visibility into upcoming auto-upgrades
assertions.reason: Provides specific gcloud command to 'Enable 72-hour advance notifications' in the monitoring section

assertions.expectation: Suggests checking the GKE release schedule for longer-range planning (~1 month best-case for new minors)
assertions.reason: While the response mentions GKE's validation pipeline and Regular channel characteristics, it doesn't specifically mention checking the GKE release schedule or provide timing estimates for new minor versions

assertions.expectation: Mentions release channel selection as the primary lever for upgrade cadence (Stable = slower, Regular = balanced)
assertions.reason: Response acknowledges the user is on Regular channel but doesn't explain the different channel options or how channel selection affects upgrade cadence
